rulings.Case Title: LORI A DUTRA VS. SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 14, 2009, line 17, DEFENDANTS MARTA MCDOWELL, CARRI LUCAS, KIMBERLY REYNOLDS, LYNN REILLY, and, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for Summ Judgment denied. Motion for Summary adjudication grt'd in part & denied in part. Mtn. grt'd as to the 2nd Cause of action for discrimination on the basis of national origin & the 5th cause of action for breach of contract. No triable issue of material fact raised. Mtn. for summ. adj. denied as to remainder. Triable issues of fact raised whether plft. is in a protected class since she was in a committed heterosexual relationship & defts regarded her as a heterosexual. A triable issue of fact raised regarding whether there was an adverse employment action that was reasonably likely to impair plft's job performance or prospects for advancement or promotion. (See Yanowitz vs. L'Oreal USA Inc. (2005) 36 Cal.4th 1028, 1054-55.) Looking at the conduct as a whole, there is a triable issue of fact regarding adverse employment action where deft's memos were placed in plft's personnel file & inhibited her changes for advancement. Triable issue of fact raised regarding nexus/animus & pretext where McDowell gave plft a positive evaluation only one month before the derogatory memo, & McDowell & Arellano's comments indicate animus (McDowell told Furminger plft was crazy and didn't know if she was straight or a dyke; Arellano snickered at plft for dating a man.) Further, the timing of the memos creates a triable issue regarding pretext: Lucas complains of an 11/07 incident in a memo sent in 3/08 & Reynold's memo, also sent in 3/08 refers to an incident in 10/07. Summ. adj. regarding governmental immunity denied. For immunity to apply, defts must show that the decisions in question are properly considered as "basic policy decisions" made at the "planning" stage of [the entity's] operations" rather than "routine duties incidental to

rulings.Case Title: CARLOS REYES VS. JULIO BONILLA et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO FRESNO COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Change Of Venues
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 14, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT JULIO BONILLA Motion For Change Of Venue. Motion for change of venue to Fresno County is granted. Motion is timely. Witnesses reside in Fresno and would be severely inconvienced by traveling to San Francisco. Actions forming the basis for this case occurred in Fresno. Ends of justice would be promoted by transferring the case to Fresno County. =(302/CWW)
